commonIssues:
Late registration requires an affidavit, notary documentation, and a fee. Beyond one year, the process requires an Order from a 1st Class Magistrate, causing significant delays.

How to fix:

  • 21 days to 1 year: Submit a Notarised Affidavit with supporting documents at the local Municipal/Gram Panchayat office with a late fee.
  • After 1 year: Obtain an order from the First Class Judicial Magistrate in your district and submit it to the local registrar.
  • Apply at WB e-District Portal or nearest CSC (Common Service Centre).
  • Helpline: 1800-345-5555 (WB e-District)

Users frequently face issues with the WB e-District or Janma-Mrityu Tathya portals, including OTP authentication failures, incorrect data display, and server errors.

How to fix:

  • Try the WB e-District Portal during off-peak hours and use Chrome browser.
  • For OTP issues, ensure your Aadhaar-linked mobile number is active and reachable.
  • If the portal is down, visit the nearest Duare Sarkar camp or DSCSC centre for assisted application.
  • Report errors at: helpdesk@wbifms.gov.in or call 1800-345-5555.

Name, age, or address discrepancies between the cremation certificate, hospital records, and ID cards cause rejection. Hospital delays in submitting Form 4/4A also block online applications.

How to fix:

  • Ensure all documents show consistent name and date before applying. If discrepancies exist, get a Notarised Affidavit explaining the difference.
  • For hospital delays in Form 4/4A submission, contact the hospital's Medical Record Officer (MRO) directly with a written request.
  • If the hospital refuses, escalate to the CMOH (Chief Medical Officer of Health) of your district.
  • District CMOH contacts: WB Health Department

West Bengal's new guidelines strictly restrict "arbitrary changes," limiting corrections to only minor, documented errors after a certificate is issued.

How to fix:

  • Prepare strong documentary evidence: hospital death record, Aadhaar, voter ID showing the correct information.
  • Submit a Notarised Affidavit specifically explaining the nature of the error (clerical, not factual).
  • Apply for correction at the Municipal Corporation / Panchayat office that issued the certificate, with the original certificate.
  • If refused, approach the District Magistrate's office for orders. Helpline: 1800-345-5555.

For lost certificates, the requirement for a police GD (General Diary) copy and a fresh application to the municipality can be time-consuming.

How to fix:

  • First file a General Diary (GD) entry at your nearest police station reporting the loss of the certificate.
  • Obtain the GD copy and apply for a duplicate certificate at the issuing Municipal Corporation or Panchayat office.
  • You can also apply online at WB e-District Portal under 'Certificate Services'.
  • Helpline: 1800-345-5555
